figures.caption: Table 2: Perplexity (↓) of perplexity-pruned models across LLaMA3, LLaMA3.1, and Qwen3 (8B). Each model is pruned by minimizing calibration perplexity on C4, then evaluated on WikiText-2 (W2), C4, and LAMBADA (LMB). M denotes the number of removed layers. The best results for each pruning scale are highlighted in bold.
Figure 2: Perplexity rank vs. accuracy rank for perplexity-pruned models. Spearman ρ is negative or near-zero in most conditions, indicating that lower perplexity does not guarantee higher accuracy.

Table 1: Zero-shot accuracy (↑) of task-likelihood-pruned models on downstream reasoning tasks for LLaMA3/3.1 and Qwen3 (8B). Each model is pruned by minimizing the task likelihood margin loss on Commonsense 170k [6], then evaluated without fine-tuning on HellaSwag (Hella), WinoGrande (Wino), ARC-Easy (ARC-E), ARC-Challenge (ARC-C), PIQA, and BoolQ. The Margin column reports the task likelihood margin (more negative is better). M denotes the number of removed layers. Best accuracy per pruning scale is in bold; best (most negative) Margin is underlined.
